introduction: This presentation focuses on the evolving trends in the North American electric vehicle sector, with a special emphasis on our competitors' strategies. The insights are derived from the 2024 report 'Trends in electric cars' on iea.org.

subsections.heading: EV Adoption Rates and Market Trends
subsections.content:
• China's dominance in EV production and battery cost efficiency, producing over 60% of global electric cars in 2023, is reshaping the competitive landscape, prompting North American automakers to reconsider their strategies.
• Major automakers like Tesla have responded by significantly reducing EV prices; Model Y saw a cut of roughly $15,000 between mid-2022 and early 2024.
• Such aggressive pricing strategies are shifting market positioning and could influence consumer adoption rates in North America ahead of 2026.
• Elexion faced operational challenges in Q3 2024, with 17% of mid-range EV orders experiencing at least a two-week delay due to supply chain issues in battery components.
• These delivery setbacks impacted Elexion's commitment to customers and could potentially affect consumer confidence and market share.
• Elexion's supply chain vulnerabilities reveal the need for a robust and responsive strategy to mitigate potential disruptions and maintain competitive positioning.
• Continued monitoring of competitive dynamics, pricing trends, and operational challenges is crucial to inform Elexion's strategic direction in the evolving North American EV market.
subsections.justification: This content provides a comprehensive view of market trends, competitive strategies, and Elexion's operational challenges, crucial to answering the DR Question.

subsections.heading: Competitor Strategies and Market Positioning
subsections.content:
• In 2023, Tesla held the largest market share in the US EV market
• General Motors announced plans to produce 30 new EV models by 2025
• Ford's Mustang Mach-E has been a successful competitor to Tesla's Model Y
• In 2023, Chinese automaker Nio entered the North American market
• Rivian's all-electric pickup truck gained significant attention in 2023
• Legacy automakers are investing heavily in EV technology to compete with Tesla and other new entrants
subsections.justification: These points discuss historical competitive strategies and positions, not future trends or positioning in 2026.

subsections.heading: Impact of Regulatory Changes on EV Market
subsections.content:
• The Biden administration announced a goal for 50% of new vehicle sales to be electric by 2030
• In 2023, Canada increased its EV incentive program to encourage adoption
• California plans to ban the sale of new gasoline-powered vehicles by 2035
• The introduction of stricter fuel economy standards in the US has boosted the EV market
• The phasing out of federal tax credits for EVs could impact future adoption rates
• The development of a unified charging standard in North America could drive EV adoption
subsections.justification: While regulatory changes impact the EV market, these points don't specifically address competitor strategies or market positioning for 2026.

conclusion: The emerging trends highlight the need for Elexion Automotive to adapt and innovate in response to our competitors' strategies. Let's discuss our next steps in capitalizing on these insights to strengthen our market positioning.
